method_atom: A self-dual (Bogomol'nyi-saturated) topological soliton solution of a two-component gauged field theory whose coupled complex order-parameter fields carry both a global non-Abelian symmetry and a local gauge symmetry spontaneously broken to a residual global symmetry. The soliton supports conserved longitudinal currents whose contribution to the total energy scales as the square of the total current, and the local gauge current integrates to zero across a transverse section. Solutions with high topological winding number develop nested, radially layered profiles in which the gauge-field flux concentrates in a shell offset from the geometric center, sandwiched between counter-flowing current-carrying layers.
object_atom: Straight current-carrying vortices in a two-component charged scalar condensate described by the Abelian Higgs model with two scalar fields (the semilocal limit of the electroweak model), where the vortex carries longitudinal electromagnetic and global currents and forms a pipelike magnetic structure.
brief_reason: The self-dual multi-field soliton with symmetry-breaking, current-squared energy scaling, and nested layered winding structure is the transferable machinery, while the two-component charged-scalar electroweak-vortex condensate is the physics-specific object; the two separate cleanly.
